codeReview.summary: The commit adds a dedicated e2e workspace and a 12-test workflow suite, but the implementation falls short of the feature contract in two important ways: it bypasses the promised CLI/MCP surfaces and it runs against live shared host state instead of an isolated sandbox.

issues.description: The suite exercises `GitOps`/`SkillOps` services directly rather than the CLI or MCP entrypoints. The feature description explicitly says this workflow should test integration across git-ops, skill-ops, and CLI/MCP, but no CLI command or MCP request is issued anywhere in the file, so regressions in the actual user-facing wiring would not be caught.

The test hard-codes the live remote repo (`~/repos/shuvbot-skills`) and active-skills directory (`~/.codex/skills`), then mutates them in place via pull/activate/deactivate steps. That violates the mission guidance to keep e2e work in isolated test directories, makes the suite non-hermetic, and it does not restore any pre-existing active state because it forcibly deactivates `test-skill` before and after the workflow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `backend-worker` skill still nudges SSH-related work toward the live `~/repos/shuvbot-skills` checkout, which conflicts with this mission's isolated-directory rule for e2e work and likely contributed to the worker hard-coding live paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:82-89` recommends `ssh shuvtest "cd ~/repos/shuvbot-skills && git status"`, while mission `AGENTS.md:16,75` says existing skills repos are read-only for e2e tests and isolated test directories must be used. The transcript skeleton also shows the worker probing `~/repos/shuvbot-skills` and `~/.codex/skills` before authoring the test.

summary: Fail: the added suite verifies package-level git/skill operations and OTEL spans, but it does not exercise the promised CLI/MCP surfaces and it mutates shared remote host state instead of using isolated e2e sandboxes.
